themes.exampleQuote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
The sage salted almond on a waffle was so good.
The owner is extremely friendly and nice and helped make the experience that much better.
The comment along the lines of 'I'm not going to waste any more ice cream on you all,' delivered in a rude tone, made us feel uncomfortable.
Add that delicious waffle to it and you go into a brand new realm of delicious.

notable_quote: I've been to Belgium several times and this place has waffles that are just as good as what you could get in Liège.
sentiment_summary: Reviewers consistently celebrate the waffle-and-ice-cream combination as something genuinely rare in Pittsburgh, with the Liège format and specific flavors like sage salted almond drawing repeat visits; the friction point is a sampling policy that at least two reviewers found communicated harshly, not as a rule but as a rebuke.
quarter_narrative: Thirty-two Pittsburgh creators posted over a million plays of Treat this quarter, and Treat posted nothing back. The gap is not abstract: @dipitcafe alone pulled nearly 297,000 plays on a single waffle-and-ice-cream video, while the shop's Instagram went silent for roughly 200 days. Meanwhile, Page's holds the top spot in the Pittsburgh Sweets family on the strength of its social momentum, the exact signal Treat has handed entirely to outside voices. At #7 in the family, Treat sits on a product that the city clearly wants to watch. What moves the rank is whether the business starts showing up in the same conversation its customers are already having without it.
tldr_read: Over a million TikTok plays from 32 creators, a dormant Instagram, and a service note that surfaces twice in the reviews. Ones to Watch, #7 in Pittsburgh Sweets.
tldr_meaning: The product signal and conversion path are fully built; the rank ceiling is the Instagram dormancy and the absence of any owned TikTok presence in a category where creator volume is already exceptional.

diagnosis_pullquote.line: Seventh In Pittsburgh Sweets, Carried By A Million Plays The Shop Didn't Post.

playbook.headline: Restart the Instagram feed, now
playbook.action: The account has a 7.65% engagement rate and has been silent for 200 days. One post reactivates an audience already primed to respond.

playbook.headline: Claim the TikTok attention on TikTok
playbook.action: Thirty-two creators generated 1.1 million plays this quarter with no owned account to absorb any of it. A single account with a repost or duet of @dipitcafe's 296,900-play video starts the loop.

playbook.headline: Post a clear samples policy in-store
playbook.action: Two separate reviews name the same friction point around tasting limits. A posted sign removes ambiguity before it becomes a pattern in the 623-review record.
